---
title: "Episode 4: How random is the NBA? — Can statistics prove that basketball is a bad sport?"
date: 2026-01-13
description: "Is the NBA just random? Basketball games are often decided by the last shot — does this mean the outcome is essentially a 50/50 coin flip?"
categories: [Maths, Statistics, Basketball, NBA]
---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
**Published**: 2026-01-13  
**Duration**: 29:15  
**YouTube**: [Watch here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sBAKID4RidY)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
**Topics Covered**: Maths, Statistics, Basketball, NBA
:::

:::

## Video

<iframe width="100%" height="400" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/sBAKID4RidY?si=oeZ7kVzfHtFawyUZ" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen></iframe>

## Overview

Welcome to the Corridor of Uncertainty, a podcast at the intersection of maths & sport. Hosted by Dr Jess Hargreaves & Dr Rich Bingham, two mathematicians at the University of York.

Is the NBA just random? Basketball games are often decided by the last shot, but both teams usually score around 100 points. Does this mean that the outcome is just decided by whoever had the ball last? This would mean the winner is basically decided on a 50/50 chance. Can stats tell us if this is really the case? Does scoring a buzzer beater mean your team is good — or just lucky?

## Key Concepts

::: {.callout-tip}
## \[Key Concept 1\]
\[Explanation of the concept\]
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## \[Key Concept 2\]
\[Explanation of the concept\]
:::

- \[Link to paper/article 1\]
- \[Link to code repository\]
- \[Link to related resource\]

> "\[Notable quote from the video\]"

\[Additional context or explanation\]

> "\[Another notable quote\]"

\[Context\]

## Code Examples

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Python

```python
# Example code from episode
def example_function():
    pass
```

## R

```r
# R equivalent if applicable
example_function <- function() {
  # code
}
```

:::

## Discussion

\[Add any additional commentary, extended explanations, or connections to other episodes\]

::: {.callout-note}
